topic: Sharp non-equivalence and strict-extension theorem between three leading interference / network spillover identification estimators that handle SUTVA violation under approximate neighborhood interference: the Approximate Neighborhood Interference (ANI) estimator of Leung (2022, Econometrica) using exposure-mapping projection at fixed neighborhood radius r, the network-cluster-robust estimator of Leung (2023, Econometrica) with HAC-style cluster-bandwidth selection, and the randomization-test estimator of Basse, Ding, Feller, Toulis (2024, Econometrica) using exact randomization-based inference under partial interference.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the sharp frontier in (rho, epsilon_M, r, b_n, design) at which fixed-radius ANI exposure IPW, network cluster-robust inference, and exact partial-interference randomization tests target the same limiting direct-plus-spillover contrast versus provably different functionals.

source_refs.claim: ANI permits nonzero effects beyond a fixed neighborhood radius and studies IPW exposure estimators with network HAC inference, but does not compare them sharply to cluster-robust or permutation-test targets.
source_refs.author: Michael P. Leung

source_refs.claim: Network cluster-robust inference is characterized by low-conductance clusters and spectral clustering, not by exposure-radius correctness.
source_refs.author: Michael P. Leung

source_refs.claim: Randomization tests for peer effects in group-formation experiments give finite-sample exact permutation inference under design-compatible exposure restrictions, without a sharp ANI/HAC equivalence frontier.
source_refs.author: Guillaume Basse, Peng Ding, Avi Feller, Panos Toulis

source_refs.excerpt: The proposal does not claim statistical inference, asymptotic coverage, or an efficient estimator
not a non-equivalence between named estimators

open_problems.why_unsolved: The three literatures control different mathematical objects: ANI controls approximate potential-outcome tails and exposure-propensity weights, network cluster-robust inference controls dependence through graph conductance and cluster partitions, and randomization tests control finite design orbits under partial-interference/group-formation restrictions. Prior ThmSmith network-exposure work explicitly stopped at population identified sets, while a failed panel sensitivity run shows that calibration thresholds are not enough for named-estimator non-equivalence. The missing bridge is a common model in which tail decay, conductance, exposure misspecification, and assignment symmetry are simultaneously parameterized.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient theorem giving inequalities in rho, epsilon_M, r, b_n, and design-orbit structure for equality of estimands, first-order scores, and asymptotic null laws, plus graph/design counterexample sequences proving strict separation outside the equality cells.

open_problems.open_problem: Find sharp bias or partial-identification bounds for ANI exposure effects when the exposure map misses an epsilon_M fraction of interference-relevant assignments, with the bound indexed by the full degree distribution rho rather than only a maximum-degree cap.

source_refs.claim: Misspecified exposure mappings can define estimable contrasts, but the paper separates estimability from the assumption that the mapping captures the full causal structure.
source_refs.author: Fredrik Sävje

source_refs.claim: Exposure mappings make arbitrary interference operational under specified exposure conditions, but do not supply sharp misspecification bounds.
source_refs.author: Peter M. Aronow, Cyrus Samii

source_refs.claim: ANI weakens exact local interference by allowing distant assignments to matter less, but does not express radius or exposure-map misspecification as a sharp finite bound.
source_refs.author: Michael P. Leung

source_refs.excerpt: there exists an open set of finite experiments... such that \Theta_I(P)\subsetneq \Theta_{\mathrm{cell}}(P)
Leung2022 replaces exact neighborhood interference by approximate neighborhood interference

open_problems.why_unsolved: Misspecification has a causal component from omitted potential-outcome arguments and a design component from exposure propensity distortion. The accepted prior network-exposure proposal gives a finite shared-completion versus cellwise-relaxation separation, but it does not translate that separation into an ANI radius calculus or a rho-indexed asymptotic frontier; degree heterogeneity can make small exposure-map error concentrate on high-leverage neighborhoods.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp bound F(rho, epsilon_M, r, tail_decay, assignment_probabilities) for the gap between the ANI exposure estimand and the target direct-plus-spillover effect, with extremal network/outcome constructions proving the bound is tight and identifying when it collapses to zero.

open_problems.open_problem: Determine whether network cluster-robust inference with cluster-bandwidth scaling b_n is a strict extension of network-HAC ANI inference, or whether the two variance procedures are incomparable across graph sequences indexed by rho and conductance.

source_refs.claim: ANI inference uses a network HAC variance estimator and interprets finite-population bias as exposure-effect heterogeneity.
source_refs.author: Michael P. Leung

source_refs.claim: Cluster-robust validity for network-dependent data is tied to low-conductance clusters, and simulations show cluster-robust methods can beat HAC when such clusters exist.
source_refs.author: Michael P. Leung

source_refs.claim: Network HAC consistency depends on the tradeoff between dependence decay and network denseness.
source_refs.author: Denis Kojevnikov, Vadim Marmer, Kyungchul Song

source_refs.excerpt: necessary-and-sufficient row-space condition for when an aggregate Honest-DiD sensitivity curve remains sharp
identify exactly which estimator or diagnostic each lacks

open_problems.why_unsolved: Cluster-robust validity is partition/conductance based, while HAC validity is graph-distance-kernel based; graph balls, spectral cuts, and bandwidth growth need not align under heavy-tailed degree distributions. The prior panel proposal supplies useful row-space/quotient diagnostic language but was rejected partly because it did not compare named estimator maps; the network setting still lacks a theorem that places the two covariance operators in an inclusion or incomparability relation.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A score-kernel or covariance-operator theorem showing either inclusion of valid regimes for one variance estimator inside the other, or two explicit graph sequences where network HAC is valid and cluster-robust fails and vice versa, parameterized by b_n, conductance, and rho.

open_problems.open_problem: Identify when partial-interference or group-formation randomization-test restrictions imply the same exposure-effect null as ANI fixed-radius exposure estimands, and when cross-cluster leakage makes the randomization null and ANI contrast non-equivalent.

source_refs.claim: Conditional randomization mechanisms yield valid tests under general interference, but the conditioning choice does not automatically identify an ANI exposure effect.
source_refs.author: Guillaume W. Basse, Avi Feller, Panos Toulis

source_refs.claim: Permutation tests for group-formation peer effects are exact for design-compatible exposure permutations, but their framework is not a sharp equivalence theorem for ANI tails.
source_refs.author: Guillaume Basse, Peng Ding, Avi Feller, Panos Toulis

source_refs.claim: Partial interference gives foundational direct and indirect effects but assumes no cross-group interference.
source_refs.author: Michael G. Hudgens, M. Elizabeth Halloran
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.excerpt: HudgensHalloran2008 define direct and indirect effects under interference in the partial-interference setting
proves a sharp finite joint-completion projection and a strict cellwise-relaxation gap

open_problems.why_unsolved: Exact randomization inference is finite and design-orbit based, whereas ANI is asymptotic and graph-tail based. Partial interference eliminates cross-cluster spillovers by assumption, while ANI permits small but nonzero cross-cluster spillovers; the prior network-exposure entry uses partial-interference terminology only as background and does not compare group-level randomization restrictions to neighborhood-radius exposure restrictions.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A strict-extension theorem showing when partial-interference clusters imply an ANI exposure mapping, when ANI implies a valid partial-interference randomization test, and when neither contains the other, with counterexamples indexed by cross-cluster edge mass, degree distribution, and randomization design.

open_problems.open_problem: Find a finite certificate basis for when unitwise exposure-misspecification relaxations are too wide because no single hidden network can realize the demanded exposure labels.

source_refs.excerpt: every strict gap... has a finite certificate supported on the capacitated incidence system
start with K_i=1 matching/cover certificates before claiming the fully capacitated b-matching case
Add the four-unit capacity witness as an explicit numerical corollary or example

open_problems.why_unsolved: The accepted prior proposal identifies shared graph completion as the novelty-bearing obstacle, but the exact certificate family remains underspecified. Matching, covering, odd-set, and lifted-cover inequalities can all become relevant once positive exposure demands, zero exposure demands, and degree caps interact; the reviewer specifically asked to scope the first target to K_i=1 before claiming the fully capacitated b-matching case.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A theorem reducing strict nonsharpness of the relaxed exposure bound to infeasibility of a signed capacitated incidence system, with a minimal certificate basis for K_i=1 and an extension or counterexample for degree-cap and rho-indexed b-matching regimes.

literature_map:
Leung 2022 Econometrica: ANI relaxes exact local interference and legitimates fixed-radius exposure IPW plus network HAC, but leaves sharp misspecification and cross-estimator frontiers open.
Leung 2023 Econometrica: cluster robustness is governed by low conductance and spectral clusters, not by exposure-radius correctness.
Basse, Ding, Feller, Toulis 2024 Econometrica: exact permutation tests for group-formation peer effects target design-compatible exposure nulls, not general ANI tails.
Savje 2024 Biometrika: misspecified exposure mappings can define estimable contrasts, creating a gap between estimability and causal interpretation.
Aronow and Samii 2017 Annals of Applied Statistics: exposure mappings make arbitrary interference operational but put hard assumptions into the exposure definition.
Basse, Feller, Toulis 2019 Biometrika and 2022 JRSSB: conditional and graph-theoretic randomization tests solve validity problems, not identification equivalence.
Hudgens and Halloran 2008 JASA; Tchetgen Tchetgen and VanderWeele 2012 SMMR: partial interference is foundational but rules out the cross-cluster leakage ANI permits.
Kojevnikov, Marmer, Song 2021 Journal of Econometrics: network HAC depends on decay-versus-density tradeoffs, matching the rho axis of the proposed frontier.
Conley 1999 Journal of Econometrics and Barrios et al. 2012 JASA: spatial HAC and clustering show the older metric-dependence tension behind network bandwidth selection.
Athey, Eckles, Imbens 2018 JASA and Eckles, Karrer, Ugander 2017 JCI: network experiments can reduce bias or compute exact p-values, but topology/design restrictions remain central.
Forastiere, Airoldi, Mealli 2021 JASA and Giffin et al. 2023 Biometrics: generalized propensity score approaches handle local or spatial spillovers but assume the relevant exposure summary.
Hu, Li, Wager 2022 Biometrika: direct and indirect effects can be defined without full exposure models, but estimator equivalence under dense network spillovers is unresolved.
Basse and Airoldi 2018 Sociological Methods & Research: arbitrary/network interference has fundamental design-based limits, motivating minimax lower bounds.
Auerbach, Guo, Tabord-Meehan arXiv 2105.03810: rooted local network configurations suggest exposure sieves, raising approximation-rate questions relative to ANI fixed radius.
prior_proposal_map:
Active panel_approx_nbhd_interference_v1 had only init/state files under doc/formalization and yielded no proposal/review signal.
pid_network_exposure_v1 was the highest-signal adjacent qid: §8 Conjectures 1-2, §10 witness route, §11 scope flags, and angle0_v4 strengthening paths all target exposure misspecification under networks.
pid_network_exposure_v1 Stage 0.5 review confirms a finite joint-completion projection and strict cellwise-relaxation gap, useful but below estimator-level ANI/HAC/randomization comparison.
panel_honest_did_phase_v1 yielded support-function/quotient-frontier machinery and reviewer warnings about needing named estimator-map comparisons, relevant to HAC bandwidth b_n frontiers.
panel_honest_did_phase_v1 README explains the failure mode: sensitivity-threshold calibration was not enough for flagship non-equivalence between named estimators.
No direct Basse-Ding-Feller-Toulis or Leung 2023 proposal was found under the permitted doc/formalization scan; partial-interference signal came through network-exposure related work and scope limits.
